Class Position

java.lang.Object
core.Position
public class Position extends Object
Represents a specific spatial coordinate on the 8x8 Kuroshiro game board.

This class is strictly immutable; once a position is instantiated, its row and column coordinates cannot be modified. Any calculations for movement generate new Position instances. The internal coordinates are 0-indexed.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Position(int row, int col)
    Constructs a new Position with the specified array coordinates.

    Retrieves the internal column index of this position.

    getRow()
    Retrieves the internal row index of this position.
    int
    hashCode()
    Computes a hash code for this position, optimizing it for use as keys within hash-based data structures like HashSet or HashMap.
    boolean
    isValid()
    Determines whether this position safely falls within the physical boundaries of the standard 8x8 board.
    Position
    offset(int dr, int dc)
    Generates a new Position offset by the specified directional vector.
    String
    toString()
    Translates the internal 0-indexed integer coordinates into standard algebraic chess notation (e.g., "a8", "e4", "h1").

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, finalize, getClass, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Position

      public Position(int row, int col)
      Constructs a new Position with the specified array coordinates.
      Parameters:
      row - the 0-indexed row coordinate (0 represents the top row, rank 8)
      col - the 0-indexed column coordinate (0 represents the leftmost column, file 'a')

  • Method Details

    • getRow

      public int getRow()
      Retrieves the internal row index of this position.
      Returns:
      the row index

    • getCol

      public int getCol()
      Retrieves the internal column index of this position.
      Returns:
      the column index

    • isValid

      public boolean isValid()
      Determines whether this position safely falls within the physical boundaries of the standard 8x8 board.
      Returns:
      true if both the row and column fall between 0 and 7 (inclusive), false if the position is out of bounds

    • offset

      public Position offset(int dr, int dc)
      Generates a new Position offset by the specified directional vector. This method is heavily utilized by pieces to calculate linear movement trajectories. Note: This method does not check if the resulting position is valid on the board.
      Parameters:
      dr - the delta or change to apply to the row direction
      dc - the delta or change to apply to the column direction
      Returns:
      a new Position instance with the vector offset applied

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Evaluates equality between this position and another object. Two positions are considered mathematically equal if they share the exact same row and column indices.
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      o - the object to compare against this position
      Returns:
      true if the given object represents the identical grid coordinate, false otherwise

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Computes a hash code for this position, optimizing it for use as keys within hash-based data structures like HashSet or HashMap.
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      the computed integer hash code based on the internal coordinates

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Translates the internal 0-indexed integer coordinates into standard algebraic chess notation (e.g., "a8", "e4", "h1").
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      the algebraic string representation of the board square
